THE LOCKER ROOM

BASE CONSTRUCTION

Installation Guide
2×4 Lumber | Plywood Runners | 1″ Wood Screws | 24″ O.C. Cross Members | 4″ Curb

To determine base size, subtract 3 inches from locker depth to create toe kick space. Cross members need to be added approximately every 24 inches on center. Build the frame with pressure-treated 2×4 lumber and 1″ wood screws; attach 1/2″×4″ plywood runners on the long edges for decking. Secure the frame to the back wall and shim level (front to back and end to end). Level the base—not each locker—front to back, side to side, and plumb. Curb height is 4″ (or per project).

  • Build the frame

    Cut pressure-treated 2×4 lumber and use 1″ wood screws to build the base frame.

  • Add decking

    Attach 1/2″×4″ plywood runners on the long edges of the frame to create a decking surface for the lockers.

  • Secure to wall

    Nail or screw the frame to the back wall and shim to level (front to back and end to end).

  • Level the base

    Level the base front to back, side to side, and plumb. Level the base rather than leveling each locker.

  • Curb height

    Build the curb to 4″ tall (or per project specifications).

Note: Sizes may vary based on project. See shop drawings for project-specific details.
